methods.method_name: Rodent Species Database Compilation and Environmental Data Integration
methods.description: This method describes the process of compiling and filtering a database of rodent species from fossil sites, creating a structured matrix, assigning species to faunal components, and integrating independent isotopic data by fitting a smoothed curve and interpolating values for each fossil site's age.

steps.description: Our database is derived from Gómez Cano 6 and considers all rodent species recorded at the studied fossil sites, including 209 species.
steps.searchable_summary: Load rodent species database from source

steps.description: In order to reduce sampling biases, this database included well sampled fossil sites, which recorded a minimum of 100 m1 + m2 + M1 + M2, following van der Meulen and Daams 18 . Only a few fossil sites with a lower sample size were allowed as they were the only representatives of poorly sampled areas or because they were part of stratigraphically important sequences.
steps.searchable_summary: Filter fossil sites based on minimum sample size of rodent teeth
outputs: Filtered rodent species database (by site sample size)

steps.description: We employed this dataset of rodent species to compile a matrix with information on the number of species and percentage of species of each genus and family in each fossil site.
steps.searchable_summary: Compile matrix of species counts and percentages per site, genus, and family
inputs: Filtered rodent species database (by site sample size)

steps.description: As in other studies 16 , we only analysed taxa that were determined at the species level in each fossil site to avoid potential noise in the data due to unidentified taxa.
steps.searchable_summary: Filter species matrix to include only species identified at the species level

steps.description: We also assigned all the species in our matrix into the faunal components (FC) defined by Gómez Cano,et al. 7 , which grouped genera with similar ecological affinities ( Supplementary Fig. 2).
steps.searchable_summary: Assign species to predefined faunal components
inputs: Faunal component definitions (Gómez Cano, et al. 7)
outputs: Species matrix with faunal component assignments

steps.description: Finally, we also compiled independent bibliographic information for isotopic data contemporary to fossil sites included in our work 7 ;
inputs: Bibliographic sources (Gómez Cano, et al. 7 and others)

steps.description: this work follows Gómez Cano, et al. 7 by fitting a smoothed curve to the isotopic information provided by Zachos, et al. 51
steps.searchable_summary: Fit a smoothed curve to global marine isotopic data (δ18O)
inputs: Global marine isotopic data (Zachos, et al. 51)

steps.description: and interpolate δ 18 O values for the age of each fossil site.
steps.searchable_summary: Interpolate δ18O values from the smoothed curve for each fossil site age
outputs: Interpolated δ18O values for each fossil site
